meaning: (noun) one's mother; the woman who gave birth to or raised a person.
(noun) one's father; the man who is a parent to a person.
(noun) a house or home; a dwelling place where a family lives.
(noun) water; a clear liquid essential for life, found in rivers, lakes, and used for drinking.
(noun) fire; a flame or burning substance that gives heat and light.
(noun) a friend; a person one knows well and trusts, often outside of family.
(noun) a book; a set of printed or written pages bound together, used for reading.
(noun) the sun; the star at the center of the solar system that gives light and warmth during the day.
(noun) the moon; the natural satellite of the Earth, visible in the night sky.
(verb/noun) to love; a deep feeling of affection or care for someone.
